explicit_text: I was born in Germany in 1866, and it was there that my early life began before my family decided to seek new opportunities. In 1882, at the age of 16, I left Germany with my parents, Franz and Maria, to start a new life in the United States. Germany is where our family's roots are, and it shaped the beginning of our journey.

That European country is where our family's roots are, and it shaped the beginning of our journey.

explicit_text: In 1882, my family and I immigrated to the United States, arriving in New York City after a long voyage on the ship Westphalia. We settled in Wisconsin, where our family would grow and establish businesses over generations. The United States became the land where our entrepreneurial spirit flourished and where my descendants continued our legacy.

explicit_text: Franz Klode was my great-great-grandfather, the patriarch who immigrated from Germany with his family in 1882. He settled with us in Whitefish Bay, Wisconsin, and worked alongside his son weaving willow baskets to sell locally. His hard work and craftsmanship laid the foundation for our family's entrepreneurial spirit.

explicit_text: Whitefish Bay, Wisconsin was one of the very first places my family settled after immigrating in 1882. We moved into a house on Spring Street, and the day after arriving, I began making willow baskets by the Menominee River to earn money. This community was where our entrepreneurial journey began and where the Klode family first put down American roots.

explicit_text: The Menominee River was right by our home in Whitefish Bay, and it was there that I cut down willow trees to weave into baskets. Selling these baskets to local housewives was my first business venture in America, back in 1882. The riverbanks provided the materials that helped me earn my first significant income and start our family's entrepreneurial legacy.

explicit_text: As I grew older, I moved to Milwaukee where I began working at the Fischer Furniture Firm. Milwaukee became the center of our family's furniture business, especially when my son Emil opened The Klode Furniture Company there in 1920. Our family’s business expanded in Milwaukee, with locations on North Broadway and Plankinton Avenue, making the city a key part of our story.

explicit_text: The Klode Furniture Company was founded by my father Emil in 1920 in Milwaukee, starting at 1020 North Broadway. It grew to occupy six floors on Plankinton Avenue and was a family-run business passed down to my grandfather James. Although it closed in 1979, its legacy lives on, even featured in the Milwaukee Public Museum’s exhibit The Streets of Old Milwaukee.

explicit_text: 1020 North Broadway in Milwaukee was the original location of The Klode Furniture Company when my father Emil opened it in 1920. This building was the starting point for our family business before we expanded to a larger space on Plankinton Avenue. Today, it serves as an academic building for the Milwaukee School of Engineering, but it holds a special place in our family history.

explicit_text: Frederick, or Fritz, Klode is my father, who inherited the handiwork and business acumen of his father, grandfather, and great-grandfather. He helped build my childhood home and even constructed the large deck with my grandfather. After earning a degree in industrial engineering from the University of Wisconsin–Madison, he started The Klode Company as a manufacturers' representative, focusing on explosion-proof enclosures.
